title: Tableau Settings Page Two-Column Layout with Tutorial Links and Active Nav Highlight
story: As a product administrator configuring Tableau settings, I want to see tutorial videos and links alongside the configuration fields and know which settings page I am on, so that I can easily complete setup and navigate settings.
description:
Enhances the Tableau settings page to match the two-column modal layout used during initial configuration. The left column displays the four Tableau credential fields. The right column embeds a YouTube tutorial video and links to two written tutorials:

- Link Tableau Personal Access Token to DataTools Pro
- Tableau Pulse Import to DataTools Pro Metrics Glossary

Also updates the left navigation sidebar to highlight the currently active settings page, providing clear wayfinding across all settings pages.
acceptance_criteria: Settings page displays a two-column layout matching the configuration modal
Left column shows all four Tableau credential fields
Right column embeds the YouTube tutorial video
Two tutorial links are displayed below the video and open in new windows
Left navigation highlights the currently active settings page
Active navigation highlighting is applied consistently across all settings pages
ux_changes: Redesigned Tableau settings page to two-column layout with embedded tutorial video and links. Added active state highlighting to the left navigation sidebar across all settings pages.
implementation_notes: Tutorial links were also updated on the popup modal. The active nav highlighting was applied globally to all settings pages for consistency.
comment_summary: Waqar completed the layout on staging and also updated the tutorial links on the popup modal. Asked whether tutorial links should remain on both the settings page and the popup.
